cluster: Source-node audit — upstream regulators for ZTL

papers.title: GIGANTEA is a co-chaperone which facilitates maturation of ZEITLUPE in the Arabidopsis circadian clock
papers.authors: Cha JY, Kim J, Kim TS, Zeng Q, Wang L, Lee SY, Kim WY, Somers DE

papers.title: Distinct roles of FKF1, GIGANTEA, and ZEITLUPE proteins in the regulation of CONSTANS stability in Arabidopsis photoperiodic flowering
papers.authors: Song YH, Estrada DA, Johnson RS, Kim SK, Lee SY, MacCoss MJ, Imaizumi T

papers.title: Structure and function of the ZTL/FKF1/LKP2 group proteins in Arabidopsis
papers.authors: Ito S, Song YH, Imaizumi T

edges.mechanism: GI acts as co-chaperone with HSP90 to facilitate ZTL maturation; GI/ZTL/HSP90 ternary complexes stabilize ZTL

evidence.title: GIGANTEA is a co-chaperone which facilitates maturation of ZEITLUPE
evidence.evidence_sentence: GIGANTEA forms a ternary complex with HSP90 and ZEITLUPE and its co-chaperone action synergistically enhances HSP90/HSP70 maturation of ZEITLUPE in vitro
evidence.claim: GI stabilizes ZTL as HSP90 co-chaperone

edges.mechanism: HSP90 chaperone binds ZTL as a client and increases its stability

evidence.title: GIGANTEA is a co-chaperone which facilitates maturation of ZEITLUPE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ZTL is an HSP90 client, and the ZTL-HSP90 interaction increases ZTL stability

edges.mechanism: ZTL LOV domain absorbs blue light, inducing conformational change that modulates substrate recognition and its interaction with GI

evidence.title: Structure and function of the ZTL/FKF1/LKP2 group proteins
evidence.authors: Ito S, Song YH, Imaizumi T
evidence.evidence_sentence: The LOV domain absorbs blue light and is important for the blue-light-dependent interaction with GIGANTEA
evidence.claim: Blue light activates ZTL via LOV domain

edges.mechanism: Photosynthesis in source leaves produces sucrose which is mobilized to sink tissues; photosynthesis-derived sugars (sucrose) function as signals regulating flowering

evidence.title: The nature of floral signals in Arabidopsis: Photosynthesis and a far-red photoresponse independently regulate flowering by increasing expression of FT
evidence.authors: King RW, Hisamatsu T, Goldschmidt EE, Blundell C
evidence.evidence_sentence: Photosynthesis in the long-day leaf regulates flowering by up-regulating FT expression, with sucrose mobilized as the primary photosynthate
